text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill and {welcome|ようこそ/歓迎|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to my class.} to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** Today's {show|番組/発表|/ʃoʊ/|This is a great TV show.} is all about {pets|ペット/愛玩動物|/pets/|Pets are fun companions.}
**DrJill:** Pets are {animals|動物|/ˈænɪməlz/|Animals live in many habitats.}
**DrJill:** They {live|住む/生きる|/lɪv/|We live in a big house.} in our home.
**DrJill:** Pets are fun and some can do {tricks|芸/曲芸|/trɪks/|Dogs can do many tricks.}
**DrJill:** Let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She {answers|答える|/ˈænsərz/|She answers the question quickly.} the question, what can pets do?
**Max:** Anna, what are you watching?
**Anna:** Funny cat videos
**Max:** People {love|愛する/大好き|/lʌv/|I love my family.} funny cat videos.
**Anna:** This video has many {likes|いいね/好き|/laɪks/|This post has many likes.}
**Anna:** This is my pet cat.
**Anna:** Her name is {Meepers|名前|/ˈmiːpərz/|Meepers is a cute name.}
**Anna:** The words my and her show {belonging|所有/帰属|/bɪˈlɔːŋɪŋ/|Belonging shows ownership.}
**Anna:** My cat belongs to me.
**Anna:** The name {Mapers|名前|/ˈmeɪpərz/|Mapers sounds like a name.} belongs to her.
**Max:** Anna, I have our question for today.
**Anna:** Great, Max.
**Anna:** Thanks!
**Anna:** What can pets do?
**Anna:** What can my pet do?
**Anna:** She {eats|食べる|/iːts/|She eats her dinner.}, she sleeps, but I have an idea.
**DrJill:** I have a question for you.
**DrJill:** Who {owns|所有する|/oʊnz/|He owns a big house.} Meepers?
**DrJill:** Anna!
**DrJill:** {Neepers|名前|/ˈniːpərz/|Neepers sounds like a name.} is her cat.
**DrJill:** Today's show has many words that show {ownership|所有権/所有|/ˈoʊnərʃɪp/|Ownership means belonging.}
**DrJill:** For example, his, her, your, our, their.
**DrJill:** Next, some friends tell us about their pets.
**Students:** This is my {pet|ペット/動物|/pet/|I love my pet dog.} blue.
**Students:** I love my pet.
**DrJill:** My dog can {sit|座る/座っている|/sɪt/|Dogs sit when trained.} {pretty|きれいに/上手に|/ˈprɪti/|She sits pretty well.}
**DrJill:** Many people have
**DrJill:** Cats and dogs, they are {common|一般的な/普通の|/ˈkɑːmən/|Common pets are friendly.} pets.
**DrJill:** But what is an {uncommon|珍しい/普通ではない|/ʌnˈkɑːmən/|An uncommon pet is rare.} pet?
**DrJill:** Add un to common
**DrJill:** And you get not common.
**DrJill:** Next, Anna shows us more.
**Anna:** Today, let's meet an uncommon pet.
**Anna:** Hi Deborah.
**Anna:** Hi Chip.
**Anna:** Hi.
**Anna:** Hello.
**Students:** Hello.
**Anna:** Nice to see you.
**Students:** This is my {pet|ペット/動物|/pet/|I love my pet dog.} bird.
**Students:** His name is Tiffy.
**Students:** And he can {talk|話す/しゃべる|/tɔːk/|Birds can talk sometimes.}
**Students:** Oh, yeah.
**DrJill:** What is Deborah's pet?
**DrJill:** Her pet is a bird.
**DrJill:** Now let's go back to Anna.
**DrJill:** Is her cat learning tricks?
**DrJill:** Let's find out.
**Anna:** Felix?
**Anna:** Our cat is {unknown|知られていない/未知の|/ʌnˈnoʊn/|An unknown person is mysterious.}
**Anna:** Let's make her known.
**Anna:** She needs to learn a {trick|芸/特技|/trɪk/|Cats can learn many tricks.}
**Students:** Mom, our cat cannot learn tricks.
**Anna:** She can, I am sure.
**Students:** I am unsure.
**Anna:** You make the {video|映像/動画|/ˈvɪdiˌoʊ/|I watch videos online.}, I teach {meepers|ペット/飼い犬|/ˈmiːpərz/|Meepers is my dog.}.
**Anna:** Meepers, {jump|跳ねる/飛び上がる|/dʒʌmp/|Dogs can jump high.}!
**Anna:** Jump!
**Anna:** {Roll over|横転する|/roʊl ˈoʊvər/|Dogs roll over for treats.} meepers, like this.
**Anna:** Beepers do this.
**Anna:** Real Real {Psych|心理学/心理|/saɪk/|Psych is a fun subject.}
**Anna:** Did she do anything?
**Anna:** Felix, did you get any {funny|面白い/おかしな|/ˈfʌni/|That was a funny joke.} video?
**Students:** Oh yeah.
**Anna:** Really?
**Anna:** Post it
**DrJill:** {Pets|ペット/愛玩動物|/pets/|Pets are family members.} can be a lot of fun.
**DrJill:** Look for {common|一般的な/普通の|/ˈkɑːmən/|Common pets include dogs.} and {uncommon|珍しい/めずらしい|/ʌnˈkɑːmən/|Uncommon pets are exotic.} pets where you live.
**DrJill:** And I'll see you again on Let's Learn English with Anna.
**Students:** Mom, our {video|映像/動画|/ˈvɪdiˌoʊ/|I made a funny video.} has ten thousand likes.
**Anna:** Meepers, {roll over|横転する|/roʊl ˈoʊvər/|Dogs roll over for treats.}.
**Anna:** Wha Felix, what did you do?
**Students:** I {posted|投稿した/アップした|/poʊst/|I posted a photo online.} our video.
**Anna:** No.
**Students:** Are you {unhappy|不機嫌な/不満な|/ʌnˈhæpi/|He looks unhappy today.}?
**Anna:** How many likes?
**Anna:** Ten thousand?
**Anna:** I am happy.
